item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't quite understand how the bacteria killed the agent. I thought they stopped being animated once you left the area?

(Furthermore, since we have more bacteria in our guts than we have cells, and they die all the time, anyone affected should get attacked all over, all the time.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this one. And it's local for me, too! Definitely going to visit sometime. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a T-Rex itself, a tyrannosaurid, something in the same family; Tarbosaurus, Albertosaurus, and Gorgosaurus are all tyrannosaurids.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because there has literally never been a dinosaur found in Ohio.

Always a first for something.

The real issue here is that Ohio lies outside of T. Rex's range. It'd be like finding an Emperor Penguin waltzing about in Peru.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Foundation operatives were brought in, and discovered an instance of SCP-2563-1 composed of a largely intact fossilized remnant of a Tyrannosaurid skeleton believed to have unearthed itself from an outcrop near the center of SCP-2563.

As an Ohioan, this sentence bugs me. Because there has literally never been a dinosaur found in Ohio. And this is set in Ohio.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mind going into detail as to why it "spooked you"? It'll be helpful for the author to know what they did right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very spooky my bones are shaking

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept is kind of bland, but the execution makes it all work, especially with the skeletal bacteria killing the Agent. I also really like that the Foundation discovered and got to name a dinosaur.

thank mr. skeltal +1 updoot for calcium

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The names are based on specific bones

Tibia and tarsal

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting concept, but I'm kind of confused by how it's specified that non-living skeletal tissue reanimates but things that are killed reanimate instantly. When a person "dies" most of their cells are going to stay alive for a while longer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You caught my attention with mention of the CVNP, because I live right the fuck next to it and it's always been a big part of my life. But the effect is far too much like another skip, a forest in Brazil, I think, with wildlife made out of organs and stuff. Overall, this doesn't stand far enough apart from it, and it's just a bunch of stuff-what-kills-dudes. And what's with those Agent nicknames?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always like skips of the "a really weird thing that simply is for no good reason" variety. They have to have that special something to pull it off, mind you, but for me this one manages it. The kicker here are the experiment logs. Not too extensive, but it makes up for that with creative and informative tests. I particularly liked test 3; it manages an old school "okay, that was both unexpected and, in retrospect, probably not such a great idea…" moment while still portraying a competent foundation taking reasonable and effective precautions against such situations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Spooky, but the skeleton liberation front by itself isn't enough to carry the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’ve got mixed feelings about this one. I like the concept, but I feel like it something is missing. The problem is that I’m not sure what’s missing and it’s driving me mad trying that I can’t quite put my finger on it.
No vote for now. If something is added to make this one stand out I’ll give it an up vote, but even if nothing is changed, I can’t bring myself to down vote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still do stand by the potential of exploration logs to look into exoskeletons and diatomic shells. That could provide a horror-ish angle that directs the story. As it is, the story is rather dispersed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's one I've been working on for a while, hope you like it!
